homepage.heroSubline: Honest tire services for all makes and models. Serving Louisville drivers since 2011 with fair pricing and reliable work.
aboutText: Founded in 2011, Legacy Tire Shop started as a small family operation in the Highlands neighborhood. Our founder, a third-generation mechanic, saw a need for transparent tire services in Louisville.
We believe in clear communication and only recommending what your vehicle truly needs. No upsells, just honest assessments from ASE-certified technicians.
As a locally owned business, we support little league teams, bourbon festival shuttles, and community safety initiatives. Your tires keep Louisville moving.

valueProps.title: Local Knowledge
valueProps.description: We understand how Louisville's seasonal weather and road conditions affect tire wear.

valueProps.title: Fair Pricing
valueProps.description: Upfront quotes with no hidden fees—we respect your budget.

valueProps.title: Same-Day Service
valueProps.description: Most repairs completed within 2 hours to minimize your downtime.

valueProps.title: Warranty Honored
valueProps.description: We work with all major tire warranties to protect your investment.

homepage.ctaSubline: Call 555-TIR-R94 or book online for prompt service from Louisville's most trusted tire specialists.

services.name: Tire Repair
services.shortDescription: Fast, reliable fixes for punctures and leaks to keep you rolling safely.
services.description:
Louisville's unpredictable roads—from pothole-riddled downtown streets to gravel backroads near distilleries—can lead to unexpected tire damage. Our certified technicians assess each puncture with care, determining whether a plug, patch, or replacement is needed.

We use industry-leading rubber cement and vulcanizing processes for lasting repairs that stand up to Kentucky's temperature swings. Most repairs take under 45 minutes, and we'll check your tire pressure sensors at no extra charge. For nails in the sidewall or large gashes, we'll give you straight advice about replacement options.
process: Step 1: Inspect tire for puncture location and size
Step 2: Remove object and prepare damaged area
Step 3: Apply rubber cement and install plug/patch
Step 4: Pressure test and balance tire

faq.question: Can all punctures be repaired?
faq.answer: No. We follow industry guidelines—damage within the tread's central 3/4 can usually be fixed, but sidewall punctures require replacement.

faq.question: How long does a repair last?
faq.answer: Properly installed patches typically last the remaining life of the tire. We warranty all repairs for 1 year.

faq.question: Do you repair run-flat tires?
faq.answer: Yes, but only if the puncture meets manufacturer specifications. We'll check your tire's specific guidelines.

services.name: Seasonal Tire Changeovers
services.shortDescription: Professional switching between all-season and winter tires for Louisville's variable climate.
services.description:
Kentucky's winter ice storms and summer heat waves demand different tire strategies. We make seasonal transitions efficient with careful mounting, balancing, and storage solutions.

For Highlands residents tackling Cherokee Park's hills or commuters crossing the Sherman Minton Bridge, proper winter traction matters. We torque lug nuts to exact specs and inspect brake components during each changeover. Summer storage includes climate-controlled protection to prevent dry rot in our humid river valley environment. Ask about our discounted storage packages when you schedule both seasonal appointments.
process: Step 1: Remove current tires and inspect wheels
Step 2: Mount seasonal tires with new valve stems
Step 3: Computer-balance all wheels
Step 4: Store off-season tires (optional)

faq.question: When should I switch to winter tires?
faq.answer: We recommend mid-November for Louisville, before the first frost. Monitor forecasts for earlier changes if needed.

faq.question: Can I reuse the same TPMS sensors?
faq.answer: In most cases yes—we'll test them during installation. Some luxury models require dedicated winter sensors.

faq.question: Do you offer mobile changeovers?
faq.answer: Yes, for an additional fee we can service fleet vehicles or multiple family cars at your home.

services.name: Precision Tire Balancing
services.shortDescription: Smoother rides through Louisville's diverse terrain with perfectly balanced wheels.
services.description:
Unbalanced tires cause premature wear and vibration—especially noticeable on Bardstown Road's brick sections or during highway speeds on I-65. Our Hunter Road Force balancer identifies even slight imbalances that cheaper machines miss.

We add precisely measured weights to counteract heavy spots, checking both static and dynamic balance. This service is crucial after hitting one of Louisville's notorious potholes or curbs. For performance vehicles heading to NCM Motorsports Park, we offer race-spec balancing with adhesive weights that withstand high G-forces. Most passenger vehicles take under 30 minutes per axle.
process: Step 1: Mount tire on balancer and spin to identify imbalance
Step 2: Determine optimal weight placement
Step 3: Apply corrosion-resistant weights
Step 4: Verify smooth rotation at highway speeds

faq.question: How often should tires be balanced?
faq.answer: Every 5,000-6,000 miles, or immediately if you notice vibration above 45 mph.

faq.question: Can balancing fix alignment issues?
faq.answer: No, but we'll alert you if we spot alignment problems during the service.

faq.question: Do you balance oversized truck tires?
faq.answer: Yes, our equipment handles everything from Smart Cars to 3/4-ton pickups.

services.name: Commercial Fleet Services
services.shortDescription: Scheduled maintenance for Louisville businesses to keep deliveries on time.
services.description:
From NuLu's restaurant suppliers to distillery tour vans, reliable tires keep Louisville commerce moving. Our fleet program offers after-hours servicing with detailed digital reports for each vehicle.

We coordinate with dispatchers to minimize downtime, often servicing multiple vehicles simultaneously at your location. Our team is DOT-certified for commercial inspections and understands Kentucky's weight regulations for delivery vehicles. Services include emergency roadside repair callouts—critical when a fleet truck blows a tire on Watterson Expressway during rush hour. Volume discounts available for contracts covering 10+ vehicles.
process: Step 1: Schedule convenient service window
Step 2: Comprehensive tread depth and pressure check
Step 3: Rotate or replace as needed
Step 4: Provide digital service records

faq.question: Do you service box trucks?
faq.answer: Yes, up to 26-foot trucks with proper bay clearance. Call ahead for larger vehicles.

faq.question: Can you match our existing tire brand?
faq.answer: We maintain relationships with all major suppliers to ensure consistency across your fleet.

faq.question: How quickly can you respond to flat tires?
faq.answer: Average 90-minute daytime response within Jefferson County for enrolled fleets.

services.name: TPMS Service & Repair
services.shortDescription: Expert diagnosis and repair of tire pressure monitoring systems.
services.description:
Louisville's temperature swings—from 90°F summer days to sudden winter cold fronts—constantly affect tire pressure readings. Faulty TPMS sensors trigger annoying dashboard lights and compromise safety.

We use OEM-grade scan tools to diagnose issues whether you drive a Corvette from the Bowling Green plant or a hybrid commuting through St. Matthews. Services include sensor replacements (with your choice of OEM or aftermarket parts), system resets, and custom programming for lifted trucks with oversized tires. Many shops overlook corroded valve stems during sensor service—we replace them as standard practice to prevent slow leaks.
process: Step 1: Scan system to identify faulty sensors
Step 2: Remove tire and extract sensor
Step 3: Install new sensor with fresh seals
Step 4: Relearn system to vehicle ECU
benefits: Prevents sensor damage during tire service

faq.question: How long do TPMS sensors last?
faq.answer: Typically 5-7 years. Cold winters drain batteries faster—we see more failures in January.

faq.question: Can I disable my TPMS system?
faq.answer: No, it's federally mandated. We can install compliant aftermarket sensors if OEM parts are too expensive.

faq.question: Why does my light come on after tire rotation?
faq.answer: The system needs resetting to recognize new positions. We include this free with any rotation service.

services.name: Custom Wheel Installation
services.shortDescription: Professional mounting and fitment guidance for aftermarket wheels.
services.description:
Louisville car enthusiasts deserve proper wheel installations whether they're upgrading a Mustang for Cruisin' Louisville or fitting winter wheels on a German import. Our technicians measure backspacing and offset precisely to avoid rubbing on suspension components.

We use nylon pry bars instead of metal tools to protect expensive rims during mounting. For lowered vehicles in the local car scene, we offer stretched tire installations with careful attention to bead seating. All installations include road force balancing and a 10-mile test drive through different road surfaces—from Old Louisville's cobblestones to smooth Gene Snyder Freeway stretches—to verify no vibrations.
process: Step 1: Verify wheel/tire compatibility with your vehicle
Step 2: Mount tires using rim-protecting equipment
Step 3: Install with correct hub-centric rings
Step 4: Test drive and adjust torque specs
benefits: Ensures proper clearance at full lock

faq.question: Can you install wheels I bought online?
faq.answer: Yes, but we'll inspect them first for defects some internet sellers overlook.

faq.question: Do you offer ceramic coating for wheels?
faq.answer: Yes—highly recommended for Louisville's brake dust and winter road salt protection.

faq.question: What's your policy on aftermarket lug nuts?
faq.answer: We only install quality brands that won't swell or strip like cheap sets often do.

services.name: Used Tire Inspections
services.shortDescription: Thorough evaluations before buying used tires to avoid hidden problems.
services.description:
With many Louisville residents shopping for affordable used tires at local wholesalers or online marketplaces, our inspections help avoid dangerous purchases. We go beyond basic tread measurements to assess internal condition.

Using depth gauges and borescopes, we check for previous repairs, belt separation, and dry rot—common issues with Kentucky's humidity swings. We'll advise if a tire's remaining life justifies its price, especially important for families budgeting in neighborhoods like Shively or Okolona. Inspections take about 15 minutes per tire and include written documentation you can use to negotiate with sellers. Some area dealers even honor our estimates for trade-in credit.
process: Step 1: Measure tread depth at multiple points
Step 2: Inspect sidewalls for cracks or bulges
Step 3: Check for uneven wear patterns
Step 4: Provide detailed condition report

faq.question: Can you tell if a tire was previously repaired?
faq.answer: Yes—we check for internal patches and plugs that might compromise safety.

faq.question: How old is too old for used tires?
faq.answer: We recommend against any tires over 6 years old, regardless of tread depth.

faq.question: Do you inspect take-off tires from new cars?
faq.answer: Absolutely—these often have minimal wear but may have been stored improperly at dealerships.

reviews.name: Marcus T.
reviews.text: Had a nail in my F-150's tire right before a trip to Mammoth Cave. Legacy got me in same-day—fixed it properly with a patch/plug combo instead of just pushing a plug in like other shops. Two years later, that repair is still holding strong.

reviews.name: Elena R.
reviews.text: As a nurse commuting to Norton Hospital in all weather, I trust Legacy with my winter/summer swaps. They store my off-season tires and always remind me when it's time to switch. Last December they spotted a worn ball joint during the changeover—probably saved me from a bigger repair.

reviews.name: Devon M.
reviews.text: These guys know their fitments. I brought in some niche JDM wheels for my Civic, and they took time to measure everything perfectly. No rubbing even when my car is fully loaded with audio equipment for car meets at Waterfront Park.

reviews.name: Sharon K.
reviews.text: After three shops couldn't figure out why my RAV4's TPMS light kept coming on, Legacy diagnosed a corroded sensor connector in 10 minutes. Only reason I'm not giving 5 stars is because they didn't have the Toyota-specific part in stock that day.

reviews.name: James P.
reviews.text: Our bakery's sprinter vans get hammered on Louisville streets. Legacy's fleet program keeps them rolling with scheduled rotations and after-hours service. Their mobile repair truck even fixed a blowout at 7 AM during Derby week—lifesavers.

reviews.name: Tiffany L.
reviews.text: Saved me from buying a set of 'like new' tires on Facebook Marketplace that had internal belt separation. The $20 inspection fee was worth avoiding what could've been a dangerous situation on I-65. Will always check here first now.
